title: SpaceX buys Cursor for 60 billion dollars
summary: Spacex acquires the artificial intelligence startup Cursor to compete with OpenAI and Anthropic after its IPO.

title: Artificial intelligence could be affecting human cognition and work
summary: Studies and analyses warn about the impact of AI on cognitive ability, the rise of 'botsitting' and consumer rejection of AI in brands.

title: Snap presents its augmented reality glasses Specs
summary: Snap launches its long-awaited AR glasses that allow superimposing digital information onto the real world, although at a high price.

title: Google Earth launches global flight simulator
summary: Google Earth incorporates a flight simulator in web browsers and adds professional features to explore the world from the air.

title: US government defends xAI's turbines on national security grounds
summary: The Department of Justice invokes national security to justify xAI's unauthorized gas turbines amid a lawsuit by the NAACP.

title: Robinhood lays off 10% of its workforce
summary: The trading platform Robinhood reduces its staff by 10% to flatten its organizational structure.

title: Microsoft restructures Xbox and closes Ninja Theory
summary: Microsoft initiates a deep change in Xbox with the departure of the director of Xbox Game Studios, the closure of Ninja Theory and the search for external funding.

title: Haier presents its new range of MiniLED and large-sized televisions
summary: Haier launches in Spain televisions with MiniLED technology and giant sizes, betting on competing not on price but on quality.

title: Android 17 now available with new multitasking features
summary: Google launches Android 17 which introduces multitasking tools and expands Gemini features, first on Pixel and then on other devices.

title: Anthropic keeps its Fable and Mythos models offline by US order
summary: Anthropic makes its Fable and Mythos models inaccessible after an order from the US government, generating tensions with the White House.

title: ChatGPT loses market share for the first time below 50%
summary: ChatGPT's popularity falls while competitors gain ground and the company faces multi-million dollar losses.

title: OpenAI and Anthropic burn money with their most expensive AI models
summary: The most costly artificial intelligence models from OpenAI and Anthropic generate losses when their usage exceeds 11% of maximum capacity.

title: How to watch the World Cup without overpaying and without delays
summary: Fans look for alternatives to follow 2026 World Cup matches on DTT or streaming, avoiding delays and finding the cheapest option.

title: Starlink's changes after IPO make fiber more attractive
summary: Spacex modifies Starlink conditions: the antenna is no longer free and a rental is introduced, while fiber optics gain advantage for second homes.

title: SpaceX's valuation briefly surpasses Amazon's
summary: Spacex reaches a valuation of 2.6 trillion dollars, momentarily surpassing Amazon, although analysts show skepticism.

title: Scientists manage to predict solar flares in advance
summary: A team of scientists develops a method to forecast solar flares, something that was almost impossible until now, improving preparation for solar storms.

title: Cisco patches actively exploited vulnerability in SD-WAN Manager
summary: Cisco publishes security updates to fix a flaw in SD-WAN Manager that is being actively exploited by attackers.

title: India orders temporary ban on Telegram over exam fraud concerns
summary: The Indian government temporarily blocks the messaging app Telegram due to its use in fraud during official exams.

title: Microsoft renews its Surface Pro and Surface Laptop with the Snapdragon X2
summary: Microsoft updates its Surface devices with the Snapdragon X2 processor, offering better performance and prices starting at 1,599 euros.

title: Netflix premieres new season of its most successful romantic drama
summary: The streaming platform releases on June 30 the new season of the series that has surpassed other great successes of the romantic genre.
